# SESSION-HANDOFF

## Active architecture proposal — ADR-097

Autonomous document generation/editing is now proposed in [`docs/ADR/097-autonomous-document-tool-and-async-rendering.md`](ADR/097-autonomous-document-tool-and-async-rendering.md). Treat it as the working architecture draft for one assistant-facing `document` tool with PDFMonkey for PDF, Gamma for presentation/PPTX, PersAI-owned `doc_id`, versioned structured source state, a separate async document render-job lane, `AssistantFile` / `fileRef` output delivery, monthly document-tool quota, existing `Admin > Tools` encrypted credential storage, and `Admin > Presets` prompt metadata integration. Do not implement keyword routing, user-facing template editing, provider-owned document source truth, mandatory v1 sandbox, or document-domain state inside `assistant_media_jobs`.

### 2026-05-18 status

ADR-097 Gamma presentation revise/slide-count follow-up: completed the bounded slice that fixed two real production bugs in the presentation tool path without re-introducing any text/regex parsing or `outputFormat` inheritance from previous versions. (1) Live evidence: a `revise_document` against a PDF presentation came back as a 40MB PPTX, because `EnqueueRuntimeDeferredDocumentJobService.enqueueRevision` and `AssistantDocumentJobService.buildRevisionSourcePayload` both silently inherited `currentSourceJson.outputFormat` when the new revision request omitted `outputFormat`. Now the revise path defaults to `pdf` for chat delivery and only emits `pptx` when the new revision request explicitly sets `outputFormat: "pptx"`; the resolved `outputFormat` is also written back into the persisted `sourceJson.outputFormat` so the version record matches the rendered output. (2) Live evidence: requests like "сделай 7 слайдов" consistently produced 3-slide decks because `RuntimeDocumentProviderAdapterService.estimateGammaCardCount` had no typed input for the user's requested slide count and instead clamped a length-heuristic into a 3–5 "compact school deck" range. Instead of regex-parsing prompts (explicitly rejected as a hack), this slice introduces a typed `targetSlideCount?: number | null` field through the full chain — `RuntimeDocumentJobRunRequest.directToolExecution.request`, `AssistantDocumentSourcePayload`, scheduler `requestPayload.sourceJson`, and the `document` tool JSON schema in `native-tool-projection.ts` — validated and clamped to `[1, 30]` everywhere it enters the system. `estimateGammaCardCount` now returns the clamped `targetSlideCount` first if present, before falling back to outline/length heuristics, and emits a structured `[gamma-presentation] resolved numCards=... targetSlideCount=... visualDensity=... themeId=...` log so future "why did Gamma get N cards?" questions can be answered from logs instead of guesswork. As part of the same cleanup, the regex-based `normalizePresentationRequest` runtime guardrails were retired: the runtime now trusts the typed contract (the model is guided by tool-description text, not by runtime text-parsing of the user message). Focused verification: `corepack pnpm -r --if-present run lint`, `corepack pnpm run format:check`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime-contract run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run test --names runtime-document-tool runtime-document-provider-adapter`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run test --names enqueue-runtime-deferred-document-job`, and `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web run test` (401/401). Next recommended step after deploy: live-run two scenarios on `persai.dev` — (1) create a presentation, then ask "переделай и добавь схемы" and confirm chat delivery is PDF (not PPTX) and the cluster log shows `[gamma-presentation] resolved numCards=...`; (2) ask "сделай 7 слайдов про фотосинтез" and confirm `[gamma-presentation] resolved numCards=7 targetSlideCount=7`.

Mobile UX slice 2 — layered pull-to-refresh for sidebar and assistant settings + horizontal-swipe rejection: completed the bounded follow-up so every visible UI layer can refresh only its own data without reloading the WebView or surfaces underneath. `PullToRefresh` now locks the gesture's primary axis after an 8px lead and ignores a primarily horizontal swipe for the rest of the gesture, which keeps an accidental side-swipe near the top of a list from triggering refresh. The base container also stopped hardcoding `h-full`; the app home now passes `h-full`, while flex-column slide-overs and the sidebar pass `flex-1`. `SlideOver` accepts an optional `onPullToRefresh` and, when provided, wraps only the scrollable body in `PullToRefresh` so the title bar stays fixed — pulling does not translate the header. `Sidebar` accepts an optional `onPullToRefresh` and, when provided, wraps only the chat-list scroll region in `PullToRefresh`; the assistant header, "New chat" button, and account footer stay still. `app-shell.tsx` wires `appData.reloadChats` into the mobile sidebar overlay so pulling inside the open sidebar refreshes only the chat list (the home dashboard underneath is not touched), and wires `appData.reload` into the assistant-settings slide-over so pulling inside the panel refreshes the settings data only (assistant lifecycle, plan, telegram, notification preference). Pointer / desktop paths are unchanged because touch events do not fire on mouse input. Focused verification: `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts app/app/_components/pull-to-refresh.test.tsx app/app/_components/slide-over.test.tsx app/app/_components/sidebar.test.tsx app/app/_components/assistant-settings.test.tsx app/app/chat/page.test.tsx` (74/74, including horizontal-swipe rejection, vertical-with-jitter still triggers, SlideOver wraps/doesn't wrap based on prop and keeps the title bar outside the PTR container, plus existing sidebar/settings regression coverage), `corepack pnpm -r --if-present run lint`, `corepack pnpm run format:check`, and `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web run typecheck`. Next recommended step after deploy: on a Capacitor device, (1) open mobile sidebar over `/app`, swipe down inside it and confirm only the chat list refreshes — the home dashboard underneath is unchanged; (2) open Assistant Settings, swipe down inside the panel and confirm settings data refreshes while the title bar stays put; (3) try a clearly horizontal swipe near the top of either surface and confirm no refresh is triggered.

Mobile UX slice 1 — hardware Back from chat returns to `/app` and app home gets touch pull-to-refresh: completed the bounded first step of the Capacitor mobile-UX track. `apps/web/app/app/chat/page.tsx` now registers a back handler through the existing `pushBackHandler` LIFO stack that calls `router.replace(buildAppRootHrefPreservingNonChatParams(searchParams))`, so Android hardware Back from any chat thread lands on `/app` instead of walking back through every prior chat in WebView history. Overlays (sidebar/modal/lightbox) still consume Back first because they push above this handler. Web/browser Back outside Capacitor is intentionally unchanged. A small `PullToRefresh` component (`apps/web/app/app/_components/pull-to-refresh.tsx`) renders its own `Loader2` spinner because Capacitor WebView disables the browser-native overscroll PTR on both iOS and Android; `AppHomePage` now wraps `HomeDashboard` in it and calls `appData.reload()` after the trigger distance. `home-dashboard.tsx` releases its own `overflow-y-auto` so the PTR container is the single scroller. Focused verification: `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts app/app/_components/pull-to-refresh.test.tsx app/app/chat/page.test.tsx` (16/16, including PTR trigger/cancel/disabled/scrolled and Back-to-`/app` with and without preserved query params), `corepack pnpm -r --if-present run lint`, `corepack pnpm run format:check`, and `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web run typecheck`. Next recommended step after deploy: install the latest WebView bundle in `persai-mobile`, open a chat thread, press Android hardware Back and confirm it lands on `/app` without traversing prior chats; then pull down on `/app` and confirm the spinner appears and the dashboard refreshes without reloading the WebView.

ADR-097 Gamma presentation quality follow-up: completed the bounded live-quality slice that closes the remaining gap between the new PDF-first presentation UX and the model/runtime inputs that still occasionally produced low-quality Gamma decks. `GammaThemePickerService` now sends a richer theme-selection brief that distinguishes the main real-world cases (school/education, investor/business, formal report/explainer, and creative/storytelling) so the picker can prefer readable, age-appropriate, trustworthy, or expressive themes based on the actual audience instead of relying on a sparse generic prompt. Model-facing `document` guidance in `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/native-tool-projection.ts` and `apps/api/prisma/tool-catalog-data.ts` now explicitly says `create_presentation` is PDF-first by default, reserves PPTX for explicit editable-export intent, and warns against `imagePolicy=text_only` / `visualDensity=text_heavy` for ordinary decks unless the user explicitly wants that style. Runtime adds a final normalization layer in `RuntimeDocumentToolService` so stale model calls cannot silently push normal presentation jobs back to `pptx`; non-explicit `text_only` / `text_heavy` school decks are normalized to a more visual/readable path before enqueue, while explicit PPTX / text-only / dense-copy requests are preserved. Focused verification: `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime exec node --import tsx --test test/runtime-document-tool.service.test.ts` and `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/gamma-theme-picker.service.test.ts`. Next recommended step after deploy: live-run two fresh Gamma decks on `persai.dev` from the same assistant — one school lesson deck and one business/investor deck — and confirm (1) the tool call defaults to PDF unless the prompt explicitly asks for PPTX, (2) school decks no longer land in `text_only` / `text_heavy` unless explicitly requested, and (3) theme-picker logs show audience-appropriate theme choices.

Telegram album turn aggregation: completed the bounded slice that treats Telegram `media_group_id` albums as one user turn instead of one assistant reply per photo. API now parses `media_group_id` in `TelegramChannelAdapterService`, appends album parts into durable `assistant_telegram_album_collectors` (`apps/api/prisma/migrations/20260518120000_telegram_album_collector/migration.sql`), returns webhook `200` immediately per part, and lets `TelegramAlbumFinalizerSchedulerService` claim quiet albums after `1500ms` and execute one inbound turn with all attachments plus the album caption through the existing Telegram turn + outbound delivery path. Single photo + caption without `media_group_id`, voice, text, and non-album documents stay on the unchanged direct webhook path; separately sent follow-up text is not auto-merged into an album. Focused verification: `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/telegram-album-collector.service.test.ts`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/telegram-album-finalizer-scheduler.service.test.ts`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/telegram-channel-adapter.service.test.ts`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm -r --if-present run lint`, and `corepack pnpm run format:check`. Next recommended step after deploy: send a 2-photo Telegram album with one caption and confirm exactly one assistant reply; then send one photo with caption and confirm behavior is unchanged.

Assistant chat hard-delete cleanup is now locally advanced through the production storage-bloat fix requested after founder observed deleted-chat files still appearing in Assistant Settings > Files but no longer opening/downloading. Root cause: `ManageWebChatListService.hardDeleteChat` deleted the chat object-storage prefix and `AssistantChatMessageAttachment` rows, then deleted the chat, but never deleted the corresponding `AssistantFile` registry rows. The fix captures attachment `assistantFileId`s before deleting the chat, filters out any file refs still referenced by other chats, lets attachment/document cascade cleanup finish, then removes only chat-local `AssistantFile` rows and best-effort deletes their object keys. This keeps shared file refs safe and prevents stale broken file rows from inflating the Files surface. Focused verification: `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/manage-web-chat-list.service.test.ts` and `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run typecheck`. Next recommended step after deploy: delete a web chat with ordinary uploads and generated document outputs, then confirm Assistant Settings > Files no longer shows broken entries from that chat and storage usage is released.

ADR-097 Gamma presentation delivery UX: completed the bounded slice that changes Gamma-backed presentations from PPTX-first to PDF-first delivery without storing duplicate binaries locally or exposing raw Gamma export URLs. API enqueue/runtime/provider flow now defaults `create_presentation` to PDF output for chat delivery, but when Gamma is used the runtime also requests a quiet companion PPTX export and persists only its provider metadata (`generationId`, `gammaId`, `exportUrl`, filename, status) under `providerStatus.companionOriginal`. API adds `AssistantDocumentOriginalDownloadService` plus `GET /api/v1/assistant/documents/:docId/download-original`, which resolves the current or requested document version, reads the saved companion metadata, streams the original PPTX from Gamma when still available, and returns an honest `GoneException` (`Original PPTX is no longer available. The PDF preview is still available.`) when the Gamma export URL has expired or no companion export exists. Web adds a BFF route at `apps/web/app/api/assistant-document/[docId]/original/route.ts`, a URL helper in `assistant-api-client.ts`, and a quiet premium secondary `PPTX` action in `chat-message.tsx` next to PDF presentation attachments so the user gets a polished "PDF in chat, optional original download" flow without seeing Gamma internals. Companion export/download handling stays presentation-only; no disk-backed duplicate PPTX persistence, no fake permanent original-download promise after Gamma link expiry, and no change to ordinary PDF/PDFMonkey document delivery. Focused verification in this slice: `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime exec node --import tsx --test test/runtime-document-provider-adapter.service.test.ts` (19/19, including the PDF-default + companion-PPTX lock test), `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/provider-gateway exec tsx test/gamma-provider.client.test.ts test/provider-image-generation.service.test.ts`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/assistant-document-original-download.service.test.ts test/media-attachment.controller.test.ts test/enqueue-runtime-deferred-document-job.service.test.ts`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts app/app/_components/chat-message.test.tsx app/app/assistant-api-client.test.ts`, `corepack pnpm -r --if-present run lint`, `corepack pnpm run format:check`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run typecheck`, and `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/provider-gateway run typecheck`. Next recommended step after deploy: create one live Gamma presentation on `persai.dev`, confirm chat receives a PDF plus the quiet `PPTX` action, download the original once while the Gamma export is fresh, then repeat later with an expired export URL and confirm the backend returns the honest "original no longer available, PDF remains" response instead of a raw Gamma failure.

ADR-097 Gamma theme picker: completed the bounded presentation-theme slice without putting the theme catalog into chat/tool prompts. API now lazy-caches Gamma standard themes in `platform_gamma_theme_catalog_cache` (~7-day TTL, refresh on miss/expiry only), runs a separate enqueue-time LLM picker against the full cached catalog via provider-gateway structured output (`document_presentation_theme_picker`), and persists the chosen `gammaThemeId` on document `sourceJson` for `create_presentation` and presentation `revise_document`. Runtime maps `gammaThemeId` → `presentationOptions.themeId`, and provider-gateway forwards `themeId` to Gamma create-generation; `themeId: null` keeps the workspace default. Prisma migration: `apps/api/prisma/migrations/20260518130000_gamma_theme_catalog_cache/migration.sql`. PROD follow-up on the same day: `GammaThemeCatalogService` now resolves the Gamma tool key through `resolveSecretValueById(TOOL_CREDENTIAL_IDS.tool_document_gamma)` instead of the wrong provider-key lookup, which fixed the live bug where presentations still rendered through Gamma but the theme catalog path logged `Gamma API key is not configured` and never reached the picker LLM. Additional live follow-up: web chat delivery now allows runtime-generated `pptx` artifacts up to `100MB` on the `tool_output_persist` path while keeping the ordinary `25MB` upload cap for user files and non-presentation artifacts, which fixes the observed Gamma success + delivery failure case where the presentation rendered but `MediaDeliveryService` rejected the `.pptx` with `File exceeds maximum size of 25MB.` Focused verification: `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/gamma-theme-catalog.service.test.ts`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/media-security-policy.test.ts`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/enqueue-runtime-deferred-document-job.service.test.ts`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/provider-gateway exec tsx test/gamma-provider.client.test.ts`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime exec node --import tsx --test test/runtime-document-provider-adapter.service.test.ts` (18/18), `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run typecheck`, and `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/provider-gateway run typecheck`. Next recommended step after deploy: create two contrasting presentations on `persai.dev` (e.g. calm school lesson vs bold investor deck) and confirm API logs show `[gamma-theme-picker] selected themeId=...`, Gamma payloads include `themeId` when the picker chooses one, and delivered `.pptx` artifacts under `100MB` now land in chat instead of failing at delivery.

ADR-097 document quality pass: completed the bounded output-quality slice for generated PDFs and Gamma presentations without changing document-domain schema, delivery truth, or provider integrations. Runtime `RuntimeDocumentProviderAdapterService` now injects a restrained editorial default on white pages (`DOCUMENT_HTML_EDITORIAL_STYLE_CSS`) and extends enhanced print CSS with stronger page-break rhythm, keep-together coverage for quotes/callouts/cards, and clearer first-element/section spacing after breaks; the HTML generation prompt now defaults to minimal-clean editorial structure (`blockquote`, `callout`, `card`) and explicitly discourages loud full-page backgrounds unless the user asks for them. Gamma shaping now defaults to `balanced` density with `medium` text amount (instead of `visual_heavy` + `brief`, which produced sparse decks), prefers fewer fuller cards for compact school/educational topics (typically 3–5 instead of always ≥6), adds anti-sparse `additionalInstructions`, and infers topic-aware visual style/audience hints from prompt/source text (food/lifestyle and school topics → illustrated storytelling; software/business → professional modern). The existing `RUNTIME_DOCUMENT_ENHANCED_PAGINATION` kill-switch remains unchanged. Focused verification in this slice: `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime exec node --import tsx --test test/runtime-document-provider-adapter.service.test.ts` (18/18, including editorial CSS, expanded pagination prompt guidance, investor-deck Gamma defaults, and compact school-deck Gamma defaults), `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/provider-gateway exec tsx test/gamma-provider.client.test.ts`, `corepack pnpm -r --if-present run lint`, `corepack pnpm run format:check`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web run typecheck`, and `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run typecheck`. Next recommended step after deploy: live-verify on `persai.dev` with (1) a multi-page PDF that includes headings, a long table, blockquote/callout blocks, and a cover section—confirm page breaks/margins look clean and headers repeat; (2) a compact school biology/ficus presentation—confirm 3–4 substantive slides instead of many empty cards; (3) an investor/business deck—confirm professional styling and denser copy than the previous sparse default.

ADR-097 additional-packages follow-up: completed the bounded slice for turning document generation into a first-class package type instead of keeping `/app/packages` media-only. The package catalog enum now includes `document` in `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma`, with rollout migration `apps/api/prisma/migrations/20260518011500_document_package_type/migration.sql`. Backend package-offer resolution in `quota-offers.ts` now includes document offers and document plan limits, while quota accounting in `track-workspace-quota-usage.service.ts` now marks document monthly quota as package-bonus-eligible so purchased document packages actually raise the effective limit in the same way as image/video packages. This keeps the founder-requested "one package system" truth instead of splitting documents onto a special-case path.

The user-facing and operator-facing surfaces are now aligned to that backend truth. `apps/web/app/app/packages/page.tsx` renders a 4th Documents card, renames the page to `Дополнительные пакеты` / `Additional packages`, and slightly tightens the large card radii. `apps/web/app/admin/plans/_components/MediaPackagesSection.tsx` now accepts/administers `document` package presets as well, and `apps/web/app/admin/ops/page.tsx` + `resolve-admin-ops-cockpit.service.ts` now show document rows inside the monthly package-tools block instead of silently filtering them out. The package checkout identity was also generalized so a document-only purchase no longer looks mislabeled: CloudPayments descriptions now read `PersAI Дополнительный пакет ...`, checkout page title copy is `Оплата дополнительного пакета` / `Pay for additional package`, and runtime quota-status guidance now tells the model that packages can cover image, video, and document limits.

Focused verification in this slice: `corepack pnpm contracts:generate`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run prisma:generate`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/plan-visibility.service.test.ts`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/read-internal-runtime-quota-status.service.test.ts`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/resolve-admin-ops-cockpit.service.test.ts`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/cloudpayments-constructor-billing-provider.adapter.test.ts`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web exec vitest run app/app/billing/checkout/[paymentIntentId]/page.test.tsx`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run lint`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web run lint`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run typecheck`, and `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web run typecheck`. Next recommended step after deploy: add real `document` package presets in `Admin > Plans`, open `/app/packages` on `persai.dev`, verify 4-card layout and tighter radii in both themes, then buy a document package and confirm the purchase appears correctly on checkout and in `Admin > Ops`.

ADR-097 admin prompt-constructor + chat polish follow-up: completed the bounded production slice for tightening the system-prompt surface on `Admin > Presets`, improving onboarding copy, reducing end-of-message action spam, and making document/file attachment chips read more clearly in chat. The prompt-constructor source of truth in `apps/api/prisma/bootstrap-preset-data.ts` is now sharper and shorter in the places that directly shape UX: the system `Response UI Contract` now makes follow-up actions explicitly optional, caps them to 1-2 short user-voice requests, and forbids assistant-voice phrasing; the `agents` governance block is tightened without changing the memory/task semantics; `Preview Character Test` now asks the assistant to introduce itself in first person during setup preview; and `Welcome / First Chat Greeting` is rewritten into a short style-forward greeting that lightly surfaces PersAI-native wow features (Telegram, PDF/PPT creation, image creation/editing, Skills, knowledge base, reminders, memory) without turning into a long feature dump. The `Admin > Presets` page itself now matches the real compiler contract better by exposing `voice_summary_line` in onboarding preset metadata and compiled preview samples, while leaving Voice DNA archetypes untouched as requested.

The tool-guidance pass was also completed on the active prompt surface. Catalog-backed model instructions in `apps/api/prisma/tool-catalog-data.ts` were tightened for `image_generate`, `image_edit`, `video_generate`, `document`, `tts`, `browser`, `scheduled_action`, `background_task`, and `files`, keeping the same behavioral rules but with clearer async/delivery honesty and less token waste. The synthetic hidden tool defaults that matter most for chat quality (`memory_write`, `knowledge_search`, `knowledge_fetch`) were also tightened in `bootstrap-preset-data.ts`. Because prompt templates are persisted in `bootstrap_document_presets`, this slice also includes `apps/api/prisma/migrations/20260518003000_prompt_constructor_polish_prod/migration.sql` so existing PROD rows pick up the revised prompt defaults instead of only fresh-db/reset paths seeing the changes.

On the web chat surface, `apps/web/app/app/_components/chat-message.tsx` now caps rendered assistant follow-up chips to 2 actions, increases spacing above attachment strips, and makes file/document attachment banners more legible in both dark and light themes through stronger container contrast, slightly roomier padding, and firmer badge styling. Focused verification in this slice: `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/compile-prompt-constructor.service.test.ts`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts app/admin/presets/page.test.tsx app/app/_components/chat-message-blocks.test.tsx app/app/_components/chat-message.test.tsx`, `corepack pnpm -r --if-present run lint`, `corepack pnpm run format:check`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run typecheck`, and `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web run typecheck`. Next recommended step after deploy: open `Admin > Presets` on `persai.dev`, verify the new preview/welcome copy on a real assistant, then run one live first-chat + generated-document flow to confirm the shortened action policy and the stronger attachment card styling feel correct with real model output.

### 2026-05-17 status

ADR-097 document versioning/editing UX follow-up: completed the bounded slice for making document-version truth visible without exposing document internals as user-facing complexity. Backend document-version persistence was already correct (`revise_document` creates a new `AssistantDocumentVersion`, delivered files are linked through `AssistantDocumentDeliveredFile`, and raw file-row deletion must not break version truth). `AssistantFileRegistryService` now decorates file records with `documentLink` metadata from delivered-file truth (`docId`, `versionId`, `versionNumber`, descriptor/status fields, `isCurrentOutput`), filters archived document outputs out of the Files list, and makes the existing `deleteAssistantFile(fileRef)` action document-aware for delivered outputs: clicking the ordinary trash button archives the `AssistantDocument` and marks all delivered chat attachments for that document as deleted, while preserving binary/version/audit truth. Document delivery also writes `documentLink` onto new canonical chat attachments. Assistant Settings > Files now shows only the current output row for a document with a quiet `vN` label instead of loud badges or duplicate old-version rows, and the same trash affordance appears as for ordinary files. Chat file chips now use a quiet premium file-type badge (`PDF`, `WORD`, `PPT`, `TXT`, etc.) instead of a download icon, plus the same small `vN` marker when document metadata is present. Focused verification: `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/assistant-file-registry.cleanup.test.ts` and `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts app/app/_components/assistant-settings.test.tsx`. Next recommended step after deploy: create a PDF, revise it, delete the current row from Files, and verify the document disappears from Files/chat surfaces without direct file-delete errors while historical document truth remains intact.

ADR-097/runtime live safety follow-up: fixed the dangerous token/context blow-up found in assistant `2f8cf38e-a6d9-4609-b83a-2b748246fcec`, chat `fef73991-fb4f-47a7-8cbe-46a6e8952401`, where the user asked the assistant to "open/read" generated `doklad_o_rastenii.pdf`. Live OpenAI logs showed the `files(read)` tool result contained raw `%PDF-1.4...` binary bytes, producing ~95,964 input tokens and triggering the web "Контекст сжат автоматически" banner (`95964 -> 96355`) even though the visible chat was small. Root cause: generic runtime `files.read` allowed non-text files, mounted the PDF into sandbox, and passed `job.content` unchanged into the model-visible tool-result JSON; the existing sanitizer preserved `content` verbatim. Runtime now keeps sandbox `files.read` text-only, but PDF/DOCX are readable through the proper shared extraction path instead of being blocked: runtime calls a new internal API `/api/v1/internal/runtime/files/extract` endpoint with the canonical `fileRef`; API downloads that `AssistantFile` from object storage, runs the bytes through the shared `DocumentExtractionService` used by Knowledge and document generation, and returns extracted text/markdown/provider/quality metadata to the model. Images/unsupported binary still return a clean unsupported diagnostic. The model-visible tool-result sanitizer also defensively replaces likely-binary files content with `[binary file content omitted from model context]` and truncates large text reads to 16k chars, so future tool-output regressions cannot consume the whole model context. Focused verification: `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime test -- runtime-files-tool.service.test.ts sanitize-tool-result-for-model.test.ts`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run typecheck`, and `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run typecheck`. Next recommended step: deploy API + runtime, then repeat the exact "Открой файл и прочитай" flow on a generated PDF and confirm OpenAI input tokens stay bounded while `files.read` returns extracted document text instead of PDF bytes.

ADR-097 live document rebuild routing follow-up: fixed the specific live failure from assistant `2f8cf38e-a6d9-4609-b83a-2b748246fcec` where the user uploaded `Новый документ (3) (1).pdf`, then asked to create a new PDF "на основе моего документа", but the accepted job had `sourceUserMessageAttachments: null` and produced a PDF that admitted the source document was not provided. Root cause was not PDF extraction: runtime only forwarded attachments from the current turn into `document` jobs, while the retry turn referenced `previous attachment #1`; additionally, "пересобери" could make the model choose `revise_document`, which API correctly treated as a PersAI-document revision and rejected with `document_not_found` when the `docId` was a file alias rather than a UUID. Runtime now resolves available document source attachments from current turn plus recent ready user-uploaded text/PDF/DOCX-like chat attachments, forwards previous attachments only when the prompt/user text explicitly references a previous/attached/source file, and coerces `revise_document` with source-file aliases/non-UUID `docId` plus source attachments into new document creation. Focused verification: runtime document tool tests, turn context hydration tests, runtime lint/typecheck, and repo format check. Next recommended live check: redeploy runtime and repeat the exact "Создай новый PDF на основе моего документа" follow-up after uploading a PDF; the resulting job should persist non-null `sourceUserMessageAttachments`, log `[document-pdf-source-attachments] ... inlinedTextFiles=1`, and the PDF should contain source content rather than the user's request text.

ADR-097 source extraction is now locally advanced through the clean shared-pipeline slice requested by the founder. The bounded item was to audit Knowledge document processing and remove runtime-owned source PDF/DOCX extraction from document generation without changing Knowledge persistence semantics. `DocumentExtractionService` now lives API-side as the shared extraction core: it owns policy loading, provider availability, local text-like extraction, local PDF parsing through the current `pdf-parse` API, local DOCX extraction through `mammoth`, quality scoring, Mistral OCR default execution, LlamaParse high-quality fallback, provider trace, and extraction metadata. `KnowledgeDocumentProcessorService` remains a behavior-compatible Knowledge facade over that service, so `KnowledgeIndexingService` and the indexing worker still receive the same normalized text/provider/quality contract and continue to own chunking, embeddings, vector replacement, and `ready` / `failed` / `needs_review` state. The document job scheduler now extracts transient user-message source attachments in API before calling runtime, via `DocumentSourceAttachmentExtractionService`, and forwards the resulting `sourceFiles[]` over `RuntimeDocumentJobRunRequest`. These transient source attachments are not persisted into user KB; they only feed the one document render job. Runtime no longer performs source attachment OCR/local PDF/DOCX extraction and no longer depends on `mammoth`; it consumes `sourceFiles[]`, logs the same source-file inline counts on the PDF path, feeds the source text into `buildPdfContentRequest`, and also includes the same extracted source text in Gamma `htmlContent` / presentation instructions. Generated-PDF validation still keeps runtime-local `pdf-parse` because that validates provider output, not source extraction. Verification in this slice: focused API tests for `knowledge-document-processor`, `document-source-attachment-extraction`, document-processing policy/settings, Knowledge indexing worker, and document scheduler; focused runtime `runtime-document-provider-adapter` tests; `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run typecheck`; `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run typecheck`; plus the required repo gate before final. Next recommended step after deploy: live-verify PDFMonkey and Gamma with the same PDF/DOCX source rebuild prompts and confirm both generated outputs use real source content; do not assume pushed code is deployed until live verification confirms it.

ADR-097 live follow-up is now locally advanced through the founder-reported PDF/DOCX source-ingestion regression and background-turn model-role routing correction. Live logs for assistant `7fd2c02c-6663-4691-977d-feb3f706836d` showed two separate issues: PDF document jobs were accepted and delivered, but the source attachment was not inlined because runtime called `pdf-parse` as a legacy function while the installed `pdf-parse@2.4.5` exposes the `PDFParse` class (`Failed to extract text from pdf attachment: pdfParse is not a function` / `[document-pdf-source-attachments] ... inlinedTextFiles=0`); DOCX upload itself succeeded, but the model-visible `document` tool description still told the model that PDF/DOCX could not be auto-extracted and should be handled through `files.read`, pushing the turn into a wrong `docId`/fallback path instead of a clean `create_pdf_document` job. Runtime now supports both legacy function-style `pdf-parse` and the v2 `PDFParse` class through one `parsePdfBuffer()` helper, so source-PDF extraction and generated-PDF validation no longer soft-fail on the current dependency shape. The `document` tool descriptor now truthfully says attached `txt/md/csv/json/html/xml/pdf/docx` sources are auto-inlined by the worker, and specifically tells the model to treat attached PDF/DOCX as source input for `create_pdf_document` unless the user explicitly targets an existing PersAI `docId`. Separately, founder-requested background model-role routing is now local truth: media/document completion and error copy use `normal_reply`, background task tool-run execution uses `premium_reply`, and `background_task_evaluation` keeps `system_tool`. Verification in this slice: `corepack pnpm -r --if-present run lint`, `corepack pnpm run format:check`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run typecheck`, and focused runtime tests for `native-tool-projection`, `runtime-document-provider-adapter`, media/document completion, and background-task evaluation. Next recommended step after deploy: repeat the same founder PDF/DOCX rebuild prompts and expect `[document-pdf-source-attachments] attachments=1 inlinedTextFiles=1 inlinedBytes>0`, with the delivered PDF containing real source content rather than fallback instructions.

### 2026-05-15 status

Sandbox `files.read`/`sandboxJob` persistence is now hardened against Postgres `22P05` when decoded file or process output contains embedded NUL (U+0000): `SandboxService` strips NUL from `files.read` return text and from all string fields written into `resultPayload` / `violationMessage` on completed, failed, blocked, stale-failure, and preflight-blocked paths, so the assistant can answer "просто прочитай файл" for PDFs without the prior `sandboxJob.update` crash. This is independent of the document-worker PDF text-extraction slice (`d71aec8f`): the document path inlines PDF text in `loadSourceAttachmentContents`; this sandbox slice fixes the generic `files` tool path. Next: deploy both runtime + sandbox images so dev picks up the fixes.

ADR-097 live follow-up is now also locally advanced through end-to-end PDF and DOCX inline text extraction on attachment ingestion, closing the founder-reported "пересборка PDF из исходника не работает + чтение файла сломалось" regression. Live evidence (founder chat trace): user attached `Новый документ (3) (1).pdf`, asked to rebuild it as a clean PDF, the model dutifully called `files` tool with `action: "read"` (the structured note from the previous attachment slice told it to do so for binary types), and the sandbox path crashed on a Postgres `22P05 unsupported Unicode escape sequence (\u0000 cannot be converted to text)` while writing the extracted PDF bytes into `sandboxJob.update().resultPayload.content` — `executeFilesReadAction` was naively calling `buffer.toString("utf8")` on raw binary PDF bytes that happen to contain NUL bytes. The model then fell back to imagination and "сделала то же самое". Root-cause fix on the document side: instead of routing `application/pdf` and `.docx` attachments through the `files.read` sandbox path (which is wrong for binary content anyway), the document worker now extracts their text directly inside `loadSourceAttachmentContents()` and inlines it into `sourceFiles[].text`, exactly the same way text-eligible MIMEs already work. Three concrete code changes in `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/runtime-document-provider-adapter.service.ts`. (1) New constant `DOCUMENT_SOURCE_ATTACHMENT_BINARY_PARSER_MIMES` is a `Map<string, "pdf" | "docx">` covering `application/pdf`, `application/x-pdf`, and `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document`; new constant `DOCUMENT_SOURCE_ATTACHMENT_PARSER_MAX_BYTES = 1_048_576` caps the raw payload size we feed into a parser so an oversized 50 MB scanned PDF cannot block the worker pod. Files above the cap surface as `text: null` with a structured note pointing at the cap and asking the user to share a smaller file. (2) `loadSourceAttachmentContents()` now branches: text-eligible MIMEs keep the existing UTF-8 inline path (unchanged, already locked by tests); MIMEs that match `DOCUMENT_SOURCE_ATTACHMENT_BINARY_PARSER_MIMES` flow into a new private method `extractInlineableAttachmentText(buffer, kind)` that requires `pdf-parse` (already a runtime dep — also reused by the existing PDF validation path) for `kind === "pdf"` and the newly added `mammoth@^1.12.0` runtime dep for `kind === "docx"`; everything else (images, archives, audio/video, unknown binary) still surfaces as `text: null` with a structured note saying "binary attachment without an inline text extractor — proceed with the user's textual prompt only". Each parser branch is wrapped in try/catch so a corrupt/encrypted/empty input surfaces as `text: null + note` rather than crashing the worker, and the result is fed through a new `normalizeInlineableAttachmentText()` (collapses runs of spaces/tabs but preserves paragraph line breaks, collapses 3+ newlines to a single paragraph break, normalizes CRLF) before being truncated to the existing `DOCUMENT_SOURCE_ATTACHMENT_MAX_INLINE_BYTES = 64_000` cap with an explicit truncation note. Empty extractor output (e.g., scanned-only / OCR-needed PDF) surfaces as `text: null` with a "may be image-only / scanned / encrypted" note rather than inlining a useless empty string. (3) The HTML generation developer-instructions block was retightened: the previous "BINARY SOURCE FILES: ... call the `files` tool with action: read" guidance is replaced with a "UNPARSEABLE SOURCE FILES" block that no longer tells the model to call `files.read` (that path is wrong for binary content) and instead tells it to read the matching `note`, briefly explain the blocker to the user (oversized / scanned / encrypted / image), and ask for a smaller / OCR-ed / unencrypted version or a paste of the relevant excerpt. Test coverage in `apps/runtime/test/runtime-document-provider-adapter.service.test.ts`: the previous "binary attachment surfaces a structured note" test was rewritten to use an `image/png` attachment instead of `application/pdf` (since PDF is no longer in the unparseable bucket), and four new locked tests were added — (a) `application/pdf` attachment with a mocked `extractInlineableAttachmentText` returning real text inlines that text into `sourceFiles[].text` with `note: null`, downloads the object exactly once, and dispatches with `kind: "pdf"`; (b) `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document` attachment dispatches with `kind: "docx"` and inlines the extracted text; (c) corrupt PDF (extractor returns `text: null + error`) surfaces as `text: null` with a structured note "Failed to extract text from pdf attachment: <error>" and never throws; (d) oversized binary (1_048_577 bytes, 1 byte over cap) refuses to invoke the parser and surfaces a "above the 1048576-byte text-extraction cap" note. The slice still does NOT touch the Gamma/PPTX worker (`runGammaPath` does not call `loadSourceAttachmentContents` — symmetric attachment support there remains a separate bounded follow-up), does NOT add image orchestration into PDFMonkey (separate bounded follow-up — image attachments currently surface as text:null + structured note, the model is told to proceed with the user's textual prompt only), and does NOT touch the sandbox `files.read` UTF-8-decode-on-binary bug — that path is no longer the document-flow critical path because PDFs and DOCXs are now extracted at the worker boundary, but the sandbox crash on NUL bytes is still a real defensive-hardening item if any other code path ever calls `files.read` on a binary file (carried as a separate bounded follow-up below). Verification in this slice: `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime exec node --import tsx --test test/runtime-document-provider-adapter.service.test.ts` (22/22 green, including 4 new attachment-extraction lock tests + 1 rewritten binary/image test), `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run lint`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run test` (full runtime suite green), `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run test` (full API suite green), `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm -r --if-present run lint`, `corepack pnpm run format:check`. Next recommended step: founder live verification on dev — attach the same PDF that triggered the original regression and ask "пересобери документ в красивый PDF"; expected `kubectl logs runtime` signal is `[document-pdf-source-attachments] attachments=1 inlinedTextFiles=1 inlinedBytes>0` and the regenerated PDF should contain real content from the source PDF, not invented placeholder text. Pending bounded follow-ups in priority order: (B) image orchestration in PDFMonkey so user-attached images become `<img src="signed-url">` in the generated PDF, (C) symmetric attachment ingestion in `runGammaPath` so DOCX/PDF/text attachments also reach Gamma as `additionalInstructions`, (D) sandbox `files.read` defensive hardening so a NUL-byte payload cannot crash `sandboxJob.update()` (Postgres `22P05`) for any code path that still calls `files.read` on raw binary content, and (E) `per_tool_cap limit=1 observed=2` so the model can call `document` twice (or `document` plus another tool) in one turn.

ADR-097 live follow-up is now also locally advanced through a post-attachment-slice cleanup pass with no behavior change. After the attachment-ingestion slice and the worker-side framing-dedup slice landed, the document lane carried five orphaned helpers and a couple of stale shim comments that were no longer truthful. (1) `RuntimeDocumentProviderAdapterService` had `parseJsonObject`, `extractJsonObjectCandidate`, `unwrapJsonCodeFence`, `renderOutline`, and `escapeHtml` left over from the deleted `document_completion_framing` worker call (the framing call now lives exclusively in `AssistantDocumentJobDeliveryService.resolveCompletionAssistantText` per the previous slice) and from an unused outline-render helper. All five private methods are now removed; the file no longer references them. The `extractHtmlFromModelOutput` doc comment was also retightened: the JSON-envelope parsing path is no longer described as a "legacy fallback for older prompts" (which is misleading — older prompts no longer exist in the active path) but as a defensive guard against models that still reflexively wrap raw HTML in a JSON object. A small stale "Note: `loadSourceAttachmentContents` lives just below this method." in-line comment that referenced the previous file layout was deleted. (2) `EnqueueRuntimeDeferredDocumentJobService.EnqueueRuntimeDeferredDocumentJobInput.sourceUserMessageAttachments` was documented as "Optional for backward compatibility with older runtime callers", which is no longer the real reason — every runtime caller of this seam already sends the field, the optionality is now retained as a defensive shim during partial-rollout windows where a stale runtime pod could still be on the previous contract for a few minutes. The comment was rewritten to say exactly that, so future readers do not assume there is a real legacy caller behind the optionality. The same service's `resolveExecutionShape` private helper had a parameter named `_requestedOutputFormat: string | null` that was never read; both the parameter and its only call-site (which was passing `input.directToolExecution.request.outputFormat ?? null` for nothing) are now removed. (3) `AssistantDocumentJobSchedulerService` had a dead `if (providerState === "not_implemented")` branch that mapped to `document_provider_not_implemented`. Both real document providers (PDFMonkey and Gamma) are now fully implemented in the active path, no production code path emits `not_implemented` provider state any more, and any future not-yet-implemented provider would fall through to the existing generic `document_artifacts_missing` failure with full provider status preservation; the dead branch is now removed. The legacy JSON-envelope HTML extraction fallback in `extractHtmlFromModelOutput` and the `RUNTIME_DOCUMENT_ENHANCED_PAGINATION` env kill-switch were intentionally **kept** — both are recent feature flags that are still earning their keep against rare model output drift and against any need to revert to legacy print-CSS in production. The duplicated `runPdfPath` / `runGammaPath` provider-status persistence shape was also intentionally **not** refactored; after the framing-dedup removal the two paths look similar in shape but diverge meaningfully on validation, retry budget, and provider error mapping, so extracting a shared helper would obscure rather than simplify. No tests were modified — the cleanup is pure dead-code removal and comment hygiene, the existing locked tests for the framing-dedup slice (worker zero-`generateText`-calls on Gamma path) and the attachment-ingestion slice (text-eligible MIME inlining + binary attachment notes) keep passing as-is. Verification in this slice: `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run test` (full runtime suite green), `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run test` (full API suite green), `corepack pnpm -r --if-present run lint`, `corepack pnpm run format:check`. Next recommended step: founder-driven live verification of the previous two deploys (`c8b85c78` framing dedup → exactly one `document_completion_framing` call per document job in the OpenAI dashboard; `56d7514e` attachments → `[document-pdf-source-attachments]` log line with `inlinedTextFiles > 0` and `inlinedBytes > 0` when a `.md`/`.txt`/`.csv` is attached and the model is asked to base the PDF on it). After the live signal lands, the next bounded item is DOCX/PDF text-extraction so binary attachments stop surfacing as `text: null + note "call files.read"`.

ADR-097 live follow-up is now also locally advanced through end-to-end attachment ingestion on the document tool. Founder asked whether the recent PDF reliability work can already support "user drops a doc/pdf/txt + a couple of images, assistant assembles a real PDF"; the answer was "the render side is ready, but the worker has no input pipeline that takes user-message attachments as source content". This slice closes that platform-level gap for text-extractable attachments and leaves a structured forward-pointer for binary types instead of inventing a half-baked DOCX/PDF text-extraction pipeline mid-slice. End-to-end shape: `RuntimeDocumentJobRunRequest` in `packages/runtime-contract/src/index.ts` now carries `attachments: RuntimeAttachmentRef[]`, mirroring the media path's contract. `EnqueueRuntimeDeferredDocumentJobService` accepts an optional `sourceUserMessageAttachments` field, validates each entry as a full `RuntimeAttachmentRef` (attachmentId / kind / objectKey / mimeType / sizeBytes / optional filename), and persists it inside the per-job `requestJson` for `create_pdf_document`, `create_presentation`, `revise_document`, and `export_or_redeliver`. The optional/nullable shape on the read path means jobs queued before this field existed still parse cleanly. `AssistantDocumentJobSchedulerService` parses `sourceUserMessageAttachments` from persisted `requestJson` and forwards it as `attachments` on the dispatched `RuntimeDocumentJobRunRequest`, so the runtime worker receives the same attachment refs the user originally sent. On the runtime side, `RuntimeDocumentProviderAdapterService` got a new private `loadSourceAttachmentContents()` method that decides per-MIME how to handle each attachment: text-eligible MIME types (the curated `DOCUMENT_SOURCE_ATTACHMENT_TEXT_MIMES` set covering `text/plain`, `text/markdown`, `text/x-markdown`, `text/csv`, `text/tab-separated-values`, `text/html`, `text/xml`, `application/xml`, `application/json`, `application/x-ndjson`, `application/javascript`, `application/x-yaml`, `application/yaml`, plus any other `text/*`) are downloaded from `mediaObjectStorage`, decoded as UTF-8, and truncated to `DOCUMENT_SOURCE_ATTACHMENT_MAX_INLINE_BYTES` with an explicit truncation note when needed. Binary attachments (`application/pdf`, `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document`, images) are intentionally **not** downloaded; they surface as `text: null` with a structured note explaining that the worker did not inline them and pointing the model at the `files` tool with `action: "read"` and the file's alias so it can fetch the text before retrying the document call. Storage misses and fetch errors also surface as `text: null` with a diagnostic note instead of crashing the worker. The PDF HTML generation path now calls `loadSourceAttachmentContents()` once per attempt, emits a `[document-pdf-source-attachments]` structured log line with `attachments=`, `inlinedTextFiles=`, and `inlinedBytes=` so future live questions about "did the attachment actually reach the model on attempt N?" can be answered from logs instead of guesswork, and feeds the collected `sourceFiles[]` into `buildPdfContentRequest`. The HTML generation prompt now has explicit developer instructions saying: "SOURCE FILES: the user attached one or more source files. Their actual text content is included in the `sourceFiles[].text` field of this prompt. When `documentRequest.prompt` requests presentation/formatting changes (colors, layout, structure), apply them to the actual source content from `sourceFiles[].text`, do not replace the content with a fresh assistant-generated draft", plus a separate guard for binary attachments. The user-message JSON the model sees now includes a `sourceFiles[]` array with `attachmentId`, `filename`, `mimeType`, `sizeBytes`, the (truncated) `text`, and the diagnostic `note`. Backward compatibility is preserved: jobs queued before this slice have an empty `attachments: []` array and behave exactly as before. The slice does NOT add a DOCX/PDF text-extraction pipeline (separate bounded follow-up), does NOT inject `<img>` orchestration into PDFMonkey for user-attached images, and does NOT touch the Gamma/PPTX worker. Test coverage: two new locked unit tests in `apps/runtime/test/runtime-document-provider-adapter.service.test.ts`. (a) `loadSourceAttachmentContents inlines text attachments via mediaObjectStorage and surfaces binary attachments with a structured note` asserts a `text/markdown` attachment is decoded from `mediaObjectStorage.downloadObject`, a binary `application/pdf` attachment is never downloaded and surfaces `text: null` with a "Binary or non-text attachment" note, and `downloadObject` is only called for text-eligible MIMEs. (b) `buildPdfContentRequest inlines sourceFiles[].text into the user prompt and adds rebuild instructions when at least one source file has text` asserts the developer instructions include the `SOURCE FILES` block pointing the model at `sourceFiles[].text` as the real content, and the actual text content (`"Original user content that must be preserved"`) is inlined verbatim into the user prompt JSON. The previously existing "pagination guidance" test was updated to pass the new `sourceFiles: []` field through `buildPdfContentRequest`. Verification in this slice: `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime exec node --import tsx --test test/runtime-document-provider-adapter.service.test.ts` (18/18 green), `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run lint`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run test` (full runtime suite green), `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run lint`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run test` (full API suite green), `corepack pnpm run format:check`.

ADR-097 live follow-up is now also locally advanced through removal of the duplicate worker-side document completion framing LLM call. Live evidence from a deployed `a2998fd3` Gamma/PPTX run (OpenAI dashboard screenshot the founder sent in chat — two `# Background Task Evaluation` completions returning two _different_ model outputs for one presentation request: "Сделала тестовую pptx: 2 слайда…" and "Собрала тестовую презентацию: 2 слайда…", chat received only the second, the first call's tokens were burnt and discarded) ruled out the previous delivery-replay duplicate as the active cause — that one was already closed in the previous slice by caching `completionAssistantText` in `providerStatusJson` and tightening `claimDueJobs` to honor `next_retry_at` for `ready_for_delivery` rows. The real remaining source of the second framing call was inside the runtime worker itself: `RuntimeDocumentProviderAdapterService` was independently issuing a `document_completion_framing` LLM call from `runGammaPath` (line 450) and from the PDF path (line 353) as part of constructing `RuntimeDocumentJobRunResult.assistantText`. The API delivery service then took that worker-framed text through `providerStatusJson.assistantText` into `resolveCompletionAssistantText`, which still called `maybeFrame` → `RuntimeDocumentJobCompletionService.complete` → a **second**, history-aware framing LLM call. Two independent framing calls per document job is wasted spend on every single document, and only the second one (the API-layer one with chat history) ever reaches the user. The worker-side call was also misusing `bundle.promptConstructor.ordinary.sections.heartbeat` as part of its `developerInstructions`; that field is explicitly documented in `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/turn-execution.service.ts` (ADR-077 follow-up note) as the dedicated **Background Task Evaluation** prompt and "must never be appended to a normal user-visible chat turn". Fix: the worker now intentionally returns `assistantText: null` from both the PDF and the Gamma paths, with explanatory inline comments at the two return sites. The now-unused `generateDocumentCompletionText` / `buildDocumentCompletionRequest` private methods and the `DOCUMENT_COMPLETION_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS` constant were deleted outright from `RuntimeDocumentProviderAdapterService`, so the code path simply does not exist anymore — no kill-switch, no env flag, no dead branch to revive accidentally. The user-facing completion message is now generated exactly once per document job, in `AssistantDocumentJobDeliveryService.resolveCompletionAssistantText`, after the document is delivered to the chat, with full chat history as context, and cached in `providerStatusJson.completionAssistantText` so any future deferRetry replay reuses the cached text instead of calling the model again (that cache landed in the previous slice and remains the second line of defense). Test coverage in this slice: the two existing `result.assistantText === "Your document draft is ready for review."` ассерты in `apps/runtime/test/runtime-document-provider-adapter.service.test.ts` were updated to `assert.equal(result.assistantText, null)` with comments explaining why the worker now returns `null`. A new locked assertion was added to the Gamma test (`generates and persists a Gamma presentation artifact`) that records every `generateText` call the worker makes and asserts `generateTextCalls.length === 0` — the Gamma worker path must not issue ANY LLM text-generation calls (no HTML generation, no completion framing); Gamma the provider does the entire content/layout work, and the framing model only runs once in the API delivery layer. Verification in this slice: `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime exec node --import tsx --test test/runtime-document-provider-adapter.service.test.ts` (16/16 green, including the new Gamma zero-LLM-calls assertion), `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run lint`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run test` (full runtime suite green), `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm run format:check`.

ADR-097 live follow-up is now also locally advanced through a mobile-only redesign of the Assistant Settings limit cards. Founder feedback was that the 3 media cards (Edit image / Create image / Video generation) and the 3 Tool-limits cards (Document generation / Media storage / Knowledge storage) looked "empty and unfinished" on a narrow viewport — they used the desktop vertical 3-slot layout at every breakpoint, so on mobile the value sat alone in the middle of a tall card with empty padding above and below the CTA chip. Two surgical changes in `apps/web/app/app/_components/assistant-settings.tsx`. (1) `LimitMetricCard` is now responsive: on `<sm` it renders as a tight horizontal row (title + value + "осталось: N" stacked on the left, "Докупить / Сменить тариф" chip pinned to the right with `shrink-0`, no `min-h` so the card hugs its content); on `sm+` it keeps the existing 3-slot vertical layout (`min-h-[6.25rem]`, label `min-h-[2rem]`, chip-slot at the bottom-right) so a row of 3 sibling cards stays visually aligned. (2) The Tool-limits inner row (Document generation + Media storage + Knowledge storage) was hard-coded to `grid grid-cols-3 gap-2` at every breakpoint, so on mobile it crammed three cards into one tight row instead of stacking vertically. It now uses `grid grid-cols-1 gap-2 sm:grid-cols-3`, matching `orderedMonthlyMediaCards` directly above. On mobile the three Tool-limits cards now stack vertically with the same compact horizontal density as the three media cards. Desktop is byte-identical. Existing `assistant-settings.test.tsx` coverage (36/36) keeps passing because the change is pure presentational CSS (no role/aria/text-content changes). Verification: web vitest 36/36 green, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web run lint`, `corepack pnpm run format:check`.

ADR-097 live follow-up is now also locally advanced through a fix for a duplicate `document_completion_framing` LLM call observed on the live cluster (OpenAI dashboard showed two identical framing requests for one document job; the chat only received one assistant message — the second framing call burned provider tokens without producing user-visible work). Two related root causes in the API document-delivery path: (1) `AssistantDocumentJobDeliveryService.deliverReadyJob` always called `resolveCompletionAssistantText` → `maybeFrame` (an LLM call) before any `deferRetry` exit branch. Any of `document_delivery_partial_recovery_pending`, `document_quota_settlement_pending`, `document_quota_settlement_ambiguous`, or `document_delivery_recovery_pending` released the claim, the scheduler re-picked the job a few seconds later, and `deliverReadyJob` ran again — producing a second identical framing call with the same `resultText`, `artifacts`, and history. (2) `AssistantDocumentJobSchedulerService.claimDueJobs` SQL respected `next_retry_at` for `queued` rows but ignored it for `ready_for_delivery` rows, so `deferRetry` (which writes `next_retry_at = NOW() + 30s` and nulls `scheduler_claim_token` / `scheduler_claim_expires_at`) was effectively a no-op on the defer window: the very next 5-second scheduler tick re-claimed the row immediately because `scheduler_claim_expires_at IS NULL`. Fix: cache the LLM-framed text in the per-job `providerStatusJson` payload as a new `completionAssistantText` field. `resolveCompletionAssistantText` now early-returns the cached value (and logs `[document-delivery-framing-cached]`) when present, and persists the freshly-framed text into the payload via `rememberFramedCompletionText` immediately after the first successful call — a deferred retry reuses the cached framing without re-calling the provider. The scheduler SQL also now requires `("next_retry_at" IS NULL OR "next_retry_at" <= NOW())` for `ready_for_delivery` rows. Two new locked unit tests in `apps/api/test/assistant-document-job-delivery.service.test.ts`: (a) "skips provider framing call when payload already carries a cached completionAssistantText (defer-retry safe)" — `maybeFrame` is NOT called when the cache is present and the chat message uses the cached text verbatim; (b) "persists framed completionAssistantText into providerStatusJson after the first framing call" — `maybeFrame` runs exactly once on the first delivery and the returned text is written back into `providerStatusJson.completionAssistantText`. Verification: full API test suite green (11/11 in the delivery suite, including both new tests), `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run lint`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run test`, `corepack pnpm run format:check`.

ADR-097 live follow-up is now also locally advanced through a backend-only PDF pagination polish slice that closes the founder ask about ragged page breaks, sliced tables, vanishing table headers after page 1, and orphan/widow lines in generated PDFs. The previous baseline print CSS injected by `RuntimeDocumentProviderAdapterService.repairHtmlDocument` only covered typography (font, headings, simple table borders), so Chromium/PDFMonkey had no print-CSS guidance and broke at whatever page boundary happened to fall in the middle of a row or section. The single legacy constant `DOCUMENT_HTML_DEFAULT_PRINT_CSS` has been split into `DOCUMENT_HTML_BASELINE_PRINT_CSS` (verbatim previous baseline, used as kill-switch fallback) and `DOCUMENT_HTML_ENHANCED_PRINT_CSS` (baseline + curated Chromium-honored pagination rules). The enhanced CSS adds `@page { size: A4; margin: 2cm 1.8cm; }` and an `@bottom-center { content: counter(page) " / " counter(pages); }` footer; `thead { display: table-header-group }` + `tfoot { display: table-footer-group }` so long-table headers and footers repeat on every printed page; `tr { page-break-inside: avoid; break-inside: avoid }` so rows do not get cut in half; `p, li { orphans: 3; widows: 3 }` to prevent lone trailing/leading paragraph lines; `h1..h6 { break-after: avoid; page-break-after: avoid }` so headings never orphan at the bottom of a page; `.cover-page, .title-page { break-after: page; page-break-after: always }` so cover/title pages always force the body onto the next page; `.card, .keep-together, .kpi, .infocard, figure, blockquote { break-inside: avoid; page-break-inside: avoid }` so small visual blocks stay on one page; and `table { table-layout: fixed; word-break: break-word }` plus `th, td { word-wrap: break-word }` so wide tables wrap cell text instead of overflowing the page. The parse5 repair pass also gained a new `promoteImplicitTheads()` step that, for any `<table>` lacking a `<thead>`, automatically wraps the first row into `<thead>` if and only if that row contains only `<th>` cells — tables with an existing `<thead>` and rows that mix `<th>` + `<td>` (probably a row with a leading header cell, not a header row) are left untouched. The whole enhanced behavior is gated by `RUNTIME_DOCUMENT_ENHANCED_PAGINATION` (default `on`); setting it to `off` / `false` / `0` reverts both the injected CSS to the legacy baseline and disables `<thead>` auto-promote, so a single env flip restores the exact pre-pagination render in production if anything visually regresses. The HTML generation prompt in `buildPdfContentRequest` has a new explicit Pagination guidance block telling the model to use `<section class="cover-page">` for cover/title pages, put long-table headers in `<thead>` with `<th>` cells, put summary/total rows as the last `<tbody>` row instead of `<tfoot>` (so they do not repeat on every page), wrap small must-stay-together blocks in `<section class="keep-together">` or `<div class="card">`, never insert manual page breaks like `<div style="page-break-before:always">`, and keep each heading and its first paragraph in the same `<section>` so headings do not orphan. A new structured `[document-pdf-html-pagination]` log line reports `paginationEnhanced` and `theadPromoted` per job/attempt so future PDF break questions can be answered from logs instead of guesswork. Five new locked unit tests in `apps/runtime/test/runtime-document-provider-adapter.service.test.ts` cover: enhanced CSS injection by default (`@page`, thead repeat, tr break-inside, orphans/widows, cover-page break-after, table-layout fixed), `<thead>` auto-promote for header-only first rows, no-op for tables with existing `<thead>` or mixed first row, env-flag kill-switch revert to legacy baseline, and prompt-side pagination guidance. Verification in this slice: full runtime test suite green (16/16 in the adapter suite, including all 5 new pagination tests), `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime run lint`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/runtime test`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/web run typecheck`, `corepack pnpm run format:check`.

ADR-097 live follow-up is now also locally advanced through a small but visible Assistant Settings localization fix on top of the previous limits UI cleanup. The Tool limits list was leaking untranslated english labels for several tools (`Background Task`, `Knowledge Fetch`, `Knowledge Search`, `Text to Speech`, `Document Generation`) in the ru UI because the `toolLimitLabels` mapping in `apps/web/app/app/_components/assistant-settings.tsx` was keyed by historical human-readable names instead of the canonical backend `toolCode` values from `apps/api/prisma/tool-catalog-data.ts` (real codes are `tts`, `memory_get`, `memory_search`, and `background_task` / `document` had no mapping entry at all). The mapping is now keyed by the canonical `toolCode` values, an explanatory comment was added so future tool additions don't repeat the mistake, and two new i18n keys were introduced (`toolLimitBackgroundTask`, `toolLimitDocument`) so all five labels now render in proper ru/en copy. Test ассерты in `assistant-settings.test.tsx` were updated from the previous english-fallback `Documents` copy to the new translated `Document generation` label.

ADR-097 live follow-up is now also locally advanced through a focused Assistant Settings limits UI cleanup driven directly by founder UX feedback on the previous quota-cleanup slice. The "PRO / Бюджет токенов" header was visually broken (date + percent floated above the bar, the bar itself carried its own label and number, and the media cards sat flush against the bar with no breathing room), so the token block is now reshaped into one coherent panel under the plan name: `[Tokens / Токены]` top-left, `[X% used / X% использовано]` top-right, a full-width progress bar in the middle, and `[Next billing <date> / Следующее списание <date>]` bottom-left + `[used / limit]` bottom-right; on plans without a token bucket at all (free / quota-less / FREE-after-cancel), the billing date copy falls back to its original position under the plan name so "Indefinite" and "Access until" remain visible. The media row under that token block now uses a fixed visual order (edit -> create -> video) instead of locale-sorting display names, and each media card carries an explicit enabled-vs-disabled state derived from `toolDailyLimits[].active` truth: enabled cards show the real `used / limit` plus a "Top up / Докупить" chip wired to `onOpenPackagesPage`, disabled cards show "Unavailable / Недоступно" plus a "Change plan / Сменить тариф" chip wired to `onOpenPricingPage`, so the user can see at a glance which media tools exist on the active plan and which require an upgrade. The Tool limits accordion is now strictly collapsed by default — the previously always-visible Documents card was confusing because it lived outside the accordion and duplicated the section header. Documents is now the first card inside the expanded accordion, sharing one compact 3-card row with Media storage and Knowledge storage; the "Активные чаты / Active chats" compact card was fully removed per UX. The shared `LimitMetricCard` now accepts an `unavailable` flag for the disabled visual state, the now-unused `LimitBar` helper and the now-stale `visibleMonthlyMediaQuotas` / `documentMonthlyQuota` derivations were removed, and `monthlyMediaBuyChip` en copy was changed from "Add packages" to "Top up" to match the on-card CTA. `assistant-settings.test.tsx` was updated to assert the new shape (Documents hidden when accordion is collapsed, Active chats no longer rendered, `video_generate` flips into the "Unavailable" state when `toolDailyLimits.active === false`, Change-plan CTA in the media row routes to `onOpenPricingPage`).

ADR-097 live follow-up is now also locally advanced through a document-tool cleanliness audit and three small-but-real follow-ups that came out of it. The model-visible `document` tool descriptor in `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/native-tool-projection.ts` was re-validated against the current runtime contract and active backend — the 4 `descriptorMode` values (`create_pdf_document`, `create_presentation`, `revise_document`, `export_or_redeliver`) and 10 input fields match exactly, and no descriptor update is needed (the recent PDF reliability rewrite changed only the internal LLM HTML-generation sub-call, not the model-facing tool surface). `prisma/tool-catalog-data.ts` for `document` is also still accurate. The audit then surfaced one real correctness bug and two cleanliness gaps: (1) `RuntimeDocumentJobCompletionService.buildCompletionKey` was hashing only `workerResult` and silently ignoring `failure`, which meant two different failure contexts on the same document job could collide on the same idempotency key and replay an older failure-framing result instead of producing the right one — the hash now includes both `mode` and `failure`; (2) `AssistantDocumentJobDeliveryService.deliverReadyJob` had two nearly-identical `failDelivery` branches in its catch block that were collapsed into one resolved-message-id path with no behavior change; (3) `assistant-document-job-failure-copy.service.ts` previously had no unit tests, and a focused `assistant-document-job-failure-copy.service.test.ts` was added that locks the locale-inference rules and both ru/en variants of the policy-aware and generic failure copy paths. The legacy `not_implemented` provider state handler in `assistant-document-job-scheduler.service.ts` was deliberately kept as a defensive safety-net for any future document provider that gets wired but not implemented. The duplicated `workerResult` / `failure` shape between `RuntimeDocumentJobCompletionRequest` and `RuntimeMediaJobCompletionRequest` in `packages/runtime-contract` was deliberately not touched here — extracting it would change generated contracts for both document and media completion lanes at once and should be a separate contract-refactor slice.

ADR-097 bounded Gamma/presentation follow-up is now locally advanced through the next quality slice: `create_presentation` no longer depends only on a generic text prompt plus Gamma-side heuristics for visual richness. PersAI now carries a minimal typed presentation visual contract end-to-end on the active path: `visualStyle`, `imagePolicy`, and `visualDensity` are accepted in the model-visible `document` schema, persisted in document source truth, forwarded through enqueue/scheduler/runtime/provider contracts, and preserved through revision/export source normalization. Runtime Gamma shaping now maps that contract onto real documented Gamma controls (`textOptions`, `imageOptions`, `additionalInstructions`, `numCards`, `cardSplit`, `cardOptions.dimensions`) instead of the old thin `themeAccent` fallback. The bounded default is now intentionally visual-forward for presentations (`visual_heavy` + image-rich Gamma options unless `text_only` is explicit), but the slice still does **not** add template/theme management, user-supplied image orchestration, a presentation planner, or any PDF-path redesign.

ADR-097 live follow-up is now also locally advanced through a full PDF generation reliability rewrite after live evidence and founder pushback exposed that the previous "stricter validation" pass was masking the real failure mode instead of fixing it. Live cluster log inspection plus full PDF-path code reading identified that the actual primary failure modes were not in PDFMonkey or the template, but in the runtime HTML generation contract and in the lack of any structured observability on the PDF lane. The model was being asked to return its HTML inside a `strict: true` JSON schema with one long `htmlContent` string field, so any realistic-sized document forced the model to fit the whole HTML into one escaped JSON string under a 4500 max-output-tokens ceiling — the string then truncated mid-document, JSON parsing either crashed or recovered to a half-complete HTML fragment, and PDFMonkey rendered a near-empty PDF the runtime then either accepted (because byte-validation passed) or blocked (because the previous over-corrected "uninspectable → invalid_output" path threw away legitimate PDFs whenever the in-process `pdf-parse` extractor failed in production). `RuntimeDocumentProviderAdapterService` now generates HTML without the JSON envelope: the model returns raw HTML directly, the runtime extracts the HTML region from anywhere in the model output (raw, markdown-fenced, or a legacy JSON envelope as a backwards-compatible fallback), and a new `parse5`-backed backend repair pass parses the fragment, auto-creates `<html>/<head>/<body>` wrappers, closes every tag the model forgot to close, injects baseline print CSS so PDFMonkey always has a styled document to render, and rejects HTML whose visible body text is too short before any provider call. The HTML output token budget went from 4500 to 16000, the max attempt count went from 2 to 3, and attempt 2+ now use an explicit RETRY MODE prompt that asks for a compact, simple, flat 1-2 page document so the retry has a real chance to succeed instead of repeating the same large failed plan. PDF validation is also now structurally honest: it explicitly checks for `%PDF-` magic and `%%EOF`, treats `pdf-parse` extraction failure as a soft warning rather than blocking delivery, and only enforces a text-content gate when extraction actually returned text — the earlier "uninspectable → invalid_output" branch is removed because it was producing false-negative blocks instead of fixing real PDF quality. The PDF lane now also emits structured `[document-pdf-start]`, `[document-pdf-html-raw]`, `[document-pdf-html-ready]`, `[document-pdf-provider-ok]`, `[document-pdf-provider-failed]`, `[document-pdf-validation-failed]`, and `[document-pdf-success]` log lines with jobId/attempt/sizes/previews, and the PDFMonkey provider client itself now logs `[pdfmonkey-create-request]`, `[pdfmonkey-create-response]`, `[pdfmonkey-card-ready]`, `[pdfmonkey-download-ok]`, and `[pdfmonkey-download-empty]` events, so future live PDF reliability questions can be answered from logs instead of guesswork. The `per_tool_cap limit=1 observed=2` conflict that prevents producing both PDF and PPTX in the same model turn is still a known residual and should be handled as a separate bounded follow-up rather than mixed into PDF render honesty.
